title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has moved from the realm of science fiction (0) into the center of our daily lives within a few decades. (9) Although the concept was first proposed in the 1950s, it only recently gained the necessary computing power to thrive. (10) Such is the speed of its development that even experts struggle to predict where the technology will be in five years. (11) Provided that ethical guidelines are strictly followed, AI has the potential to solve some of humanity's most complex problems. (12) Not only can it analyze vast datasets in seconds, but it can also assist in medical diagnoses and climate modeling. (13) Regardless of the concerns about job displacement, the integration of AI into the workforce appears to be an inevitable process. (14) Not until the advent of deep learning did machines begin to mimic human cognitive functions with such accuracy. (15) Despite the challenges, many believe that AI will ultimately enhance human creativity rather than replace it entirely. (16) Whether we are prepared for the social changes this technology brings remains to be seen by future generations. The ongoing conversation about AI's role in society is essential for ensuring it serves the common good.

example.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has moved from the realm of science fiction (0) into the center of our daily lives.
example.explanation: Preposition showing movement or transition to a new state.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis followed by an inversion.
Conjunction meaning 'if' or 'on the condition that'.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did machines begin').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
